database_mapping.source_grain: one raw hosp.patients row per subject_id
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained std_sex row per subject_id

admin_demographic_build_translation.identifier_translation: retain subject_id directly from hosp.patients; no encounter or stay expansion is performed
admin_demographic_build_translation.value_normalization_translation: normalize MIMIC raw gender F to female and M to male; unexpected source values fail the build rather than being force-mapped
admin_demographic_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained row means the source patient table did not provide a governed sex value; it does not authorize downstream imputation

validation_contract.no_row_policy_check: absence of row is not a valid downstream category and should not be interpreted as unknown without source-specific review
notes: MIMIC raw source field is named gender, but the retained public variable is std_sex because the reviewed source behaves as a binary administrative sex-like category.
This patient-level asset should not be reinterpreted as admission-level or ICU-stay-level without an explicit join-grain rule.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint should read this mapping spec and delegate to the current local reviewed implementation.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_sex outputs for MIMIC-IV-3.1 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path.
